expectations.text: Mentions App Connection as a prerequisite for the sync
expectations.evidence: "Before you can sync secrets, you need to create an authenticated connection to AWS. This is a one-time setup" and "Step 1: Create an AWS App Connection"

expectations.text: Correctly identifies mapping behavior options (one-to-one vs many-to-one) which is specific to AWS SM
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states 'Mapping Behavior' with options: one-to-one (Each Infisical secret becomes a separate secret in AWS) and many-to-one (All secrets packed into a single AWS secret as a JSON object)

expectations.text: Recommends or mentions Key Schema for scoping managed secrets
expectations.evidence: "Key Schema (Strongly Recommended)" section with template "INFISICAL_{{secretKey}}" and explanation 'Prevents Infisical from managing secrets it didn't create'

expectations.text: Explains initial sync behavior options (overwrite vs import)
expectations.evidence: "Initial Sync Behavior" table with Overwrite Destination, Import (Prioritize Infisical), and Import (Prioritize AWS) options clearly explained

expectations.text: Mentions region configuration for the AWS destination
expectations.evidence: "Region" field mentioned: 'us-east-1 (as you specified)' in the configuration table

expectations.text: References auto-sync capability (secrets propagate on change)
expectations.evidence: "Auto-Sync (Default: Enabled)" section states 'Any change to secrets in Infisical automatically syncs to AWS within seconds'

expectations.text: Correctly states that GitHub does NOT support importing secrets (only overwrite-destination is available)
expectations.evidence: "GitHub repository secrets have a key limitation: GitHub doesn't support importing secrets back into Infisical. This means you can't directly read your existing GitHub secrets" and "overwrite-destination as the initial behavior (the only option GitHub supports)"

expectations.text: Recommends backing up or manually exporting existing GitHub secrets before syncing
expectations.evidence: "Step 1: Inventory Your Existing Secrets - Before creating any sync, document all your existing GitHub secrets" and "manually add all your existing GitHub secrets into Infisical"

expectations.text: Mentions repository scope option for GitHub sync destination
expectations.evidence: Lists GitHub scope options: 'For Repository Secrets: Scope: repository, Repository: Specify as owner/repo'

expectations.text: Mentions App Connection prerequisite (GitHub Connection)
expectations.evidence: "Step 2: Set Up Your GitHub App Connection" and "Create an App Connection to authenticate Infisical with GitHub"

expectations.text: Recommends Key Schema or Disable Secret Deletion to protect manually-managed secrets
expectations.evidence: "Key Schema: Highly recommended — use something like INFISICAL_{{secretKey}}" and "Disable Secret Deletion: Consider enabling this if you have secrets in GitHub that you're not syncing from Infisical yet"

expectations.text: Warns that existing GitHub secrets not in Infisical may be overwritten
expectations.evidence: "Any secrets not in Infisical at sync time risk being overwritten or deleted" and "overwrite-destination as the initial behavior (the only option GitHub supports)"

expectations.text: Correctly warns that Azure Key Vault converts underscores to hyphens (DATABASE_URL becomes DATABASE-URL)
expectations.evidence: "Azure Key Vault does not allow underscores in secret names. When you sync secrets from Infisical to Azure Key Vault, all underscores are automatically converted to hyphens. Example mappings: DATABASE_URL → DATABASE-URL, API_KEY → API-KEY"

expectations.text: Lists required permissions: secrets/list, secrets/get, secrets/set, and secrets/recover
expectations.evidence: Table clearly lists all four permissions: 'secrets/list' (check what exists), 'secrets/get' (read values), 'secrets/set' (create/update), 'secrets/recover' (recover soft-deleted)'

expectations.text: Recommends Key Vault Secrets Officer role as the appropriate built-in role
expectations.evidence: "Recommended Role: Key Vault Secrets Officer — This built-in Azure role includes all the permissions above and is specifically designed for secret management operations."

expectations.text: Explains why secrets/recover is needed (Azure soft-delete behavior)
expectations.evidence: "Why secrets/recover Matters: Azure Key Vault uses soft-delete by default, meaning deleted secrets remain recoverable for a retention period. If Infisical attempts to update a secret that was recently deleted, it needs the secrets/recover permission to restore it first before updating."

expectations.text: Mentions App Connection prerequisite
expectations.evidence: "Create an Azure App Connection in Infisical with your service principal credentials"

expectations.text: Mentions the vault base URL as a required destination config field
expectations.evidence: "Destination: Select your Key Vault's base URL (e.g., https://my-vault.vault.azure.net)"
